hooks.prompt: Session starting. If the PayIt2 MCP server is available, prepare context now: (1) Call get_organizer_profile to get the organizer's name and Stripe Connect status. (2) Call list_campaigns to find all active campaigns. (3) For any campaign past 50% of its timeline OR below 30% of goal/capacity, call get_suggestions to surface the top AI-recommended action. (4) Call get_conversation_history for each campaign — if there is a coaching session from the past 7 days, note the top recommendation from last time. Output a brief greeting (3-5 lines max) covering: urgent campaign alerts by name with the top suggestion, and what was last discussed if applicable. If no campaigns exist, greet warmly and suggest /campaign to get started. If MCP is unavailable or no API key is configured, output nothing.

update_campaign is about to be called. Review the tool arguments and show the organizer a clear before/after summary of exactly what fields will change. Ask them to confirm before proceeding. If they have already confirmed in this conversation (said 'yes', 'go ahead', 'do it', 'confirm', or similar), output: APPROVED. Otherwise output: NEEDS_CONFIRMATION and list each field change as: [field]: '[old value]' → '[new value]'.

An agent is being launched in this session. Check the conversation for an active campaign (a campaign title, ID, or PayIt2 URL was mentioned). If found, output a compact 3-line context block: campaign name and type, current progress (amount raised / tickets sold / members paid), and days remaining. This context gets injected into the agent so it doesn't have to re-ask. If no campaign is active in this session, output: NO_ACTIVE_CAMPAIGN

Review this conversation. If it included PayIt2 campaign coaching via /check-in, /promote, /engage, or /campaign: (1) state the campaign name and type, (2) list the top 3 specific recommendations made (be concrete — not 'improve your description' but 'rewrite the first paragraph to lead with the medical diagnosis'), (3) note any content that was generated. Also call log_event with event_type 'coaching_session_complete' and the campaign ID if known. Output a concise markdown summary covering those 3 items. If this was NOT a coaching session, output exactly: SKIP
